column1: Comprehensive Database of Jesus's Life Episodes

column2: Summary
column4: Variations Between Accounts
column6: Cultural Significance

column2: Angel Gabriel announces to Mary she will conceive Jesus
column4: Matthew: focuses on Joseph's perspective (1:18-25); Luke: details Mary's experience
column6: Central to Christian understanding of incarnation

column2: Pregnant Mary visits Elizabeth; John leaps in womb
column4: Only in Luke; includes the Magnificat (Mary's song)
column6: Important for Marian devotion; establishes relationship between John and Jesus

column2: Jesus born in humble setting, laid in manger
column4: Luke: emphasizes census, stable/manger; Matthew: mentions birth briefly
column6: Central Christian narrative; basis for Christmas celebrations

column2: Angels announce birth to shepherds who visit Jesus
column4: Only in Luke
column6: Emphasizes divine announcement to common people

column2: Jesus circumcised on eighth day
column4: Only in Luke
column6: Shows Jesus' Jewish identity and legal observance

column2: Jesus presented in Jerusalem; Simeon and Anna prophesy
column4: Only in Luke
column6: Demonstrates fulfillment of Messianic expectations

column2: Wise men follow star, bring gifts, worship infant Jesus
column4: Only in Matthew
column5: Reign of Herod the Great, ~6-4 BCE
column6: Basis for Epiphany celebrations; symbolizes recognition by Gentiles

column2: Joseph takes Mary and Jesus to Egypt to escape Herod
column4: Only in Matthew
column6: Fulfillment of prophecy; parallel to Moses

column2: Herod kills male children in Bethlehem
column4: Only in Matthew
column6: Portrays opposition to Messiah from birth

column2: Family returns after Herod's death, settles in Nazareth
column4: Matthew: emphasizes Egypt connection; Luke: direct return to Nazareth without Egypt
column6: Fulfillment of prophecy; establishes Nazareth connection

column2: Twelve-year-old Jesus debates with teachers in Temple
column4: Only in Luke
column6: Only canonical story from Jesus' youth; shows early wisdom

column2: Jesus baptized in Jordan River; heavens open
column4: Matthew: John's objection; Mark: simple account; Luke: during prayer; John: emphasizes John's testimony
column6: Central to Christian baptismal theology; divine affirmation

column2: Satan tempts Jesus during 40-day fast
column4: Matthew/Luke: detailed three temptations in different order; Mark: brief mention
column6: Model for Christian resistance to temptation

column2: Jesus calls fishermen to follow him
column4: Matthew/Mark: simple call; Luke: miraculous catch; John: Andrew & John first
column6: Foundation of apostolic ministry; model of discipleship

column2: Jesus turns water into wine
column4: Only in John
column6: First sign in John; demonstrates power over creation

column2: Jesus drives out money changers
column4: Synoptics: during Passion Week; John: at beginning of ministry
column6: Statement against corruption of worship; assertion of authority

column2: Pharisee visits Jesus at night; born again discussion
column4: Only in John
column6: Important for Christian teaching on spiritual rebirth

column2: Jesus meets Samaritan woman, reveals himself as Messiah
column4: Only in John
column6: Breaking ethnic and gender barriers; model for evangelism

column2: Extended teaching including Beatitudes and Lord's Prayer
column4: Luke: shorter "Sermon on the Plain" (6:17-49)
column6: Central Christian ethical and spiritual teaching

column2: Jesus heals Roman officer's servant from distance
column4: Matthew: centurion comes; Luke: sends Jewish elders
column6: Shows power over distance; faith of Gentile

column2: Jesus raises dead young man at funeral procession
column4: Only in Luke
column6: Demonstration of power over death; compassion
column7: Raising of Jairus' Daughter, Raising of Lazarus

column2: Jesus commands storm to cease on Sea of Galilee
column4: Minor variations in disciples' reaction
column6: Shows authority over natural elements

column2: Jesus exorcises legion of demons into swine
column4: Matthew: two demoniacs; Mark/Luke: one man
column6: Power over spiritual forces; mission to Gentiles

column2: Jesus raises girl from death; heals woman with bleeding
column4: Matthew: compressed account; Mark/Luke: detailed narrative
column6: Power over death; ritual impurity

column2: Jesus multiplies five loaves and two fish
column4: Minor variations in setting and dialogue
column6: Only miracle in all four gospels; eucharistic symbolism

column2: Jesus walks on Sea of Galilee during storm
column4: Matthew: Peter attempts to walk; absent in Luke
column6: Demonstration of divine power; Peter's faith/doubt

column2: Gentile woman's daughter healed after faith exchange
column4: Matthew: "Canaanite"; Mark: "Syrophoenician"
column6: Shows expansion to Gentiles; faith rewarded

column2: Jesus revealed in glory with Moses and Elijah
column4: Luke: during prayer; minor variations in dialogue
column6: Divine confirmation; connection to Moses/Elijah

column2: Jesus tells parable of unlikely compassionate hero
column4: Only in Luke
column6: Defining Christian compassion story; ecumenical symbol

column2: Sisters host Jesus; Martha works while Mary listens
column4: Only in Luke (different from John 11-12)
column6: Teaches priority of discipleship over busyness

column2: Jesus raises four-day dead man
column4: Only in John
column6: Greatest sign in John; catalyst for plot against Jesus

column2: Blind beggar healed near Jericho
column4: Matthew: two blind men; Mark/Luke: one (Bartimaeus)
column6: Faith rewarded; symbolic of spiritual sight

column2: Tax collector climbs tree to see Jesus; transformation
column4: Only in Luke
column6: Example of repentance; inclusion of outcasts

column2: Jesus enters Jerusalem on donkey amid crowds
column4: Luke: adds Jesus weeping over Jerusalem
column6: Fulfillment of Zechariah prophecy; messianic claim

column2: Woman anoints Jesus with expensive perfume
column4: Matthew/Mark: unnamed woman; John: Mary of Bethany
column6: Preparation for burial; act of devotion

column2: Final meal with disciples; institution of Eucharist
column4: Synoptics: focus on bread/wine; John: foot washing, discourse
column6: Foundation for Christian communion; covenant theology

column2: Jesus washes disciples' feet as example
column4: Only in John
column6: Model of humble service; cleansing symbolism

column2: Extended teaching and prayer after supper
column4: Only in John
column6: Central Johannine Christology; Trinitarian teaching

column2: Jesus prays in agony before arrest
column4: Luke: sweating blood; John: brief mention only
column6: Example of submission to divine will; human/divine tension

column2: Jesus taken by temple guards after Judas' betrayal
column4: John: soldiers fall back; Peter cuts ear
column6: Voluntary surrender; rejection of violent resistance

column2: Peter denies knowing Jesus three times
column4: John: two settings; others slightly different sequences
column6: Human weakness; contrast to Jesus' faithfulness

column2: Jesus before Annas, Caiaphas, Sanhedrin
column4: Different emphases on which Jewish authorities
column6: Religious rejection of messianic claims

column2: Jesus before Pilate (and Herod in Luke)
column4: Luke: includes Herod; John: extended dialogue
column6: Political dimension of crucifixion; Roman/Jewish tensions

column2: Jesus executed on cross outside Jerusalem
column4: Different emphases on sayings from cross
column6: Central event of Christian faith; atonement theology

column2: Sayings of Jesus from the cross
column4: Different Gospels record different sayings
column6: Deeply influential in Christian devotion and art

column2: Jesus' body placed in Joseph of Arimathea's tomb
column4: John: adds Nicodemus bringing spices
column6: Fulfillment of prophecy; verification of death

column2: Women find tomb empty; angel announces resurrection
column4: Variations in which women, number of angels
column6: Foundation of resurrection faith; women as first witnesses

column2: Risen Jesus appears to Mary Magdalene
column4: John: detailed dialogue; Mark: brief mention
column6: First resurrection witness; "apostle to the apostles"

column2: Jesus walks with two disciples, revealed in breaking bread
column4: Only in Luke
column6: Eucharistic symbolism; gradual recognition

column2: Jesus appears to disciples, shows wounds
column4: Luke: emphasizes physicality; John: giving of Holy Spirit
column6: Verification of bodily resurrection; commissioning

column2: Doubting disciple convinced by seeing Jesus' wounds
column4: Only in John
column6: Faith without seeing praised; dealing with doubt

column2: Jesus meets disciples by lake; miraculous catch
column4: Only in John
column6: Echo of earlier call; verification of resurrection

column2: Jesus reinstates Peter with "feed my sheep"
column4: Only in John
column6: Restoration after denial; pastoral commission

column2: Final instructions to make disciples of all nations
column4: Different emphases and wording
column6: Foundation for Christian mission; ongoing presence

column2: Jesus taken up to heaven before disciples
column4: Luke/Acts: most detailed; absent in Matthew/John
column6: Completion of earthly ministry; enthronement
